engineIntelligence.h2: Jeep Compass Engines - Codes, Common Failures & Replacement Costs
engineIntelligence.description: The cards below cover the main engines fitted to the Jeep Compass in the UK, including documented failure causes, variant production years, and typical rebuilt engine costs based on current UK market data. All prices reflect typical UK market ranges for supply-only rebuilt units.

cards.altText: Jeep Compass 55284064 engine - diesel 2.0L replacement UK
knownFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear - documented failure between 60k-100k miles causing rattling noise.
EGR valve clogging - common on short-journey diesel use, leads to reduced power and DPF issues.

cards.altText: Jeep Compass 55263069 engine - petrol 1.4L replacement UK
knownFailures: MultiAir hydraulic unit failure - documented between 40k-70k miles, causes misfires and loss of power.
Timing chain stretch - metallic rattling on cold start, requires specialist diagnosis.

cards.altText: Jeep Compass 46346020 engine - diesel 1.6L replacement UK
knownFailures: DPF regeneration failure - common on urban driving patterns, leads to limp mode.
Turbo actuator sticking - documented on higher-mileage units, causes boost loss.

commonProblems.h2: Jeep Compass Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Jeep Compass reliability landscape is dominated by the 2.0L MultiJet diesel and 1.4L MultiAir petrol engines, with timing chain and hydraulic unit failures driving most replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this section as a decision tool to compare repair vs replacement costs.

problems.h4: Jeep Compass Timing Chain Failure - Why 2.0 MultiJet Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner wear allows chain stretch, causing rattling on cold start and potential valve timing errors if ignored.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Chain Kit - Replace chain, guides, tensioner before failure
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove timing cover, replace chain assembly with upgraded tensioner, reset ECU adaptation
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before rattling starts; prevents catastrophic engine damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed engine, fit rebuilt unit with reinforced timing components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended once rattling is audible or engine has suffered timing-related damage

problems.recommendation: On a Jeep Compass worth £8,000-£15,000, preventative timing chain replacement at 60k miles offers better value than waiting for failure. If rattling is already present, budget for a rebuilt engine - continued driving risks valve damage.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 2.0 MultiJet engine prices for your Jeep Compass

problems.h4: Jeep Compass MultiAir Unit Failure - Why 1.4L Petrol Engines Lose Power
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic solenoid in MultiAir unit fails, preventing variable valve timing control and causing misfires, rough idle, and loss of power.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - MultiAir Unit Replacement - Replace hydraulic control unit only
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove intake manifold, replace MultiAir hydraulic module, reset adaptation values
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if engine hasn't suffered valve damage; requires specialist calibration equipment

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded MultiAir
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed engine, fit rebuilt unit with reinforced MultiAir components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if misfires have caused catalytic converter damage or valve wear

problems.recommendation: If your Compass shows MultiAir-related fault codes but no misfires, unit replacement may suffice. If rough running or catalytic warnings are present, a rebuilt engine with upgraded components offers better long-term value.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 1.4 MultiAir Jeep Compass engine replacement

problems.h4: Jeep Compass DPF & EGR Issues - Why Diesel Engines Enter Limp Mode
problems.rootCause: Short-journey urban driving prevents DPF regeneration; soot buildup clogs EGR valve, triggering limp mode and reduced power.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - DPF Clean & EGR Service - Chemical clean and component service
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove DPF for off-car cleaning, clean EGR valve and cooler, reset adaptation values
repairOptions.longevity: Viable if caught before permanent soot damage; requires regular motorway driving post-service

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - DPF Replacement - Fit new or refurbished DPF unit
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed DPF, fit refurbished unit with new sensors, perform forced regeneration cycle
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if chemical cleaning fails or DPF is physically damaged

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Engine - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with emissions components
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove engine with clogged emissions system, fit rebuilt unit with new DPF/EGR, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended if engine has suffered turbo damage from restricted exhaust flow

problems.recommendation: For Compass diesels used primarily for short trips, preventative DPF cleaning every 30k miles extends component life. If limp mode is frequent, budget for DPF replacement before turbo damage occurs.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt MultiJet diesel engine prices for your Jeep Compass

problems.h4: Jeep Compass Hybrid System Faults - Why e-Hybrid & 4xe Units Need Specialist Care
problems.rootCause: 48V belt-starter generator or high-voltage battery management faults trigger hybrid system warnings, disabling electric assist and reducing efficiency.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Hybrid Component Service - Replace BSG or DC-DC converter
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Diagnose hybrid fault codes, replace faulty 48V component, recalibrate battery management system
repairOptions.longevity: Viable for isolated electrical faults; requires hybrid-certified technician and specialist tools

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt Hybrid Powertrain - Supply & fit rebuilt unit with upgraded electronics
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Remove failed hybrid powertrain, fit rebuilt unit with upgraded BSG/battery management, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Recommended for repeated hybrid faults or battery degradation affecting EV range

problems.recommendation: Hybrid Compass models require specialist diagnostics - generic garages often misdiagnose electrical faults as engine failures. If hybrid warnings persist after software updates, seek a hybrid-certified rebuilder for accurate assessment.
problems.cta: Enquire about Jeep Compass hybrid engine replacement specialists

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Jeep Compass
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you're facing timing chain rattles, MultiAir misfires, DPF warnings, or hybrid system faults, EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Jeep Compass specialists. Every rebuilt engine includes upgraded components addressing the original failure point, plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty and nationwide delivery. Compare quotes today and get back on the road without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Jeep Compass Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Jeep Compass and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

types.title: Jeep Compass Reconditioned Engines (Recon)

types.frontDescription: Professionally cleaned, inspected and lightly rebuilt units with worn parts replaced - ideal for budget-conscious Compass owners.
types.frontDisclaimer: Suitability depends on engine code and failure history - verify compatibility first.
types.backDescription: Reconditioned Compass engines undergo basic refurbishment - ideal for low-mileage failures or preventative replacement. Best for 1.6 MultiJet or early 1.4 MultiAir units.

types.title: Jeep Compass Rebuilt Engines

engineSizes.h2: Jeep Compass Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every displacement fitted to the Jeep Compass in the UK is listed below, with compatible engine codes, production years and common failure notes to help you identify the right replacement.

items.title: Jeep Compass 1.6L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 1.6L MultiJet II entered the Compass range in 2019 as an entry-level diesel option for FWD variants, offering improved fuel economy for urban drivers but with less towing capability than the 2.0L units.
commonFailurePoints: DPF regeneration failure on short-journey use; turbo wastegate sticking on higher-mileage units
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Compass 1.6L diesel engines

items.title: Jeep Compass 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0L MultiJet II has been the backbone of the Compass diesel range since 2017, available in 140ps and 170ps outputs for both FWD and AWD configurations, dominating UK replacement enquiries.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear 60k-100k miles; EGR valve clogging on urban driving patterns
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Compass 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Jeep Compass 1.3L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.3L Firefly T4 replaced the 1.4L MultiAir from 2020, offering improved emissions compliance and hybrid compatibility while maintaining responsive performance for urban and motorway use.
commonFailurePoints: Turbo wastegate sticking on higher-mileage units; carbon buildup on intake valves
items.cta: Compare Jeep Compass 1.3L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Compass 1.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 1.4L MultiAir was the Compass petrol flagship at launch, available in 140ps and 170ps outputs, offering strong performance but with known hydraulic unit vulnerabilities driving replacement demand.
commonFailurePoints: MultiAir hydraulic unit failure 40k-70k miles; timing chain stretch causing cold-start rattling
items.cta: Compare Jeep Compass 1.4L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jeep Compass 2.4L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.4L Tigershark carried over from the MK49 generation for early MP/552 models, offering simple naturally-aspirated reliability for US-spec and early UK imports before being phased out.
commonFailurePoints: Timing chain tensioner wear on higher-mileage units; oil consumption increase after 80k miles
items.cta: Compare Jeep Compass 2.4L petrol engine prices

groups.title: Jeep Compass Hybrid / MHEV Engine Sizes

items.title: Jeep Compass 1.5L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The 1.5L T4 e-Hybrid introduced mild-hybrid technology to the Compass range from 2021, improving efficiency through 48V electric assist but adding electrical complexity requiring specialist servicing.
commonFailurePoints: 48V belt-starter generator failure on early units; DC-DC converter faults triggering hybrid warnings
items.cta: Enquire about Jeep Compass 1.5L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Jeep Compass 1.3L Hybrid Engines
items.description: The 1.3L 4xe plug-in hybrid combines petrol and electric power for 240hp combined output, offering EV-only range for urban commuting but with complex service requirements and higher replacement costs.
commonFailurePoints: High-voltage battery degradation reducing EV range; power electronics cooling faults on high-mileage units
items.cta: Enquire about Jeep Compass 1.3L hybrid engine availability

items.description: Diesel engines dominate Compass replacement enquiries, with the 2.0L MultiJet II covering most UK variants from 2017-present and the 1.6L MultiJet II added for FWD models from 2019.
items.descriptor: Diesel engines dominate Compass replacement enquiries, with the 2.0L MultiJet II covering most UK variants from 2017-present and the 1.6L MultiJet II added for FWD models from 2019.

items.descriptor: Petrol Compass engines span the 1.3L-2.4L range, with the 1.4L MultiAir dominating early replacement enquiries and the 1.3L Firefly T4 taking over from 2020 for improved emissions.

families: 55263069 - 1.4L MultiAir 140ps, high failure rate due to hydraulic unit, dominant replacement demand

46338979 - 1.3L Firefly T4 130ps, replacement for MultiAir from 2020, improved reliability profile

Availability more limited than standard engines - allow longer lead times

engineYears.h2: Jeep Compass Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Jeep Compass production timeline (2007-present), organised so UK owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from specialists.

years.description: The original Compass launched with Chrysler-sourced petrol engines and VW/Mercedes diesel options for European markets.
keyChanges: Launch with 2.0L/2.4L World Engine petrol and 2.0L VW TDI diesel
2011 facelift introduced revised styling and improved interior
2.2L Mercedes OM651 diesel replaced VW unit for Euro 5 compliance
knownFor: Simple naturally-aspirated petrol engines with good parts availability
Early diesel units prone to injector and turbo issues on high-mileage examples
MK49 platform shared with Dodge Caliber and Jeep Patriot
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2016 Jeep Compass replacement engines

years.preview: MP/552 Launch: MultiJet II & MultiAir Arrive
years.description: The all-new MP/552 Compass introduced Fiat-sourced MultiJet diesel and MultiAir petrol engines, dominating UK replacement demand today.
keyChanges: New platform shared with Jeep Renegade, improved interior and technology
1.4L MultiAir petrol and 2.0L MultiJet II diesel became core UK powertrains
AWD available on select variants with Selec-Terrain system
knownFor: MultiAir hydraulic unit failures driving early replacement enquiries
2.0L MultiJet timing chain concerns emerging on higher-mileage examples
Strong demand for AWD diesel variants in UK market
years.cta: Get quotes for 2017-2019 Jeep Compass replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Compass 1.4 MultiAir engine replacement • Jeep Compass 2.0 MultiJet timing chain • Jeep Compass Trailhawk engine cost

years.description: The 2020 facelift refreshed styling and introduced the 1.3L Firefly T4 petrol and first hybrid options for the Compass range.
keyChanges: Revised front-end styling and updated Uconnect infotainment
1.3L Firefly T4 replaced 1.4L MultiAir for improved emissions compliance
1.5L e-Hybrid MHEV and 1.3L 4xe PHEV launched for electrified options
knownFor: Hybrid variants require specialist servicing and diagnostics
1.3L Firefly showing improved reliability vs earlier MultiAir units
PHEV battery degradation concerns emerging on early 2020 examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2020-2021 Jeep Compass replacement engines
years.ticker: Jeep Compass 1.5 e-Hybrid engine • Jeep Compass 4xe PHEV replacement • Jeep Compass hybrid system fault

years.description: Latest Compass models feature updated emissions compliance, refined hybrid systems and continued support for core MultiJet II diesel engines.
keyChanges: Euro 6d emissions compliance across all powertrains
Hybrid system software updates improving reliability
Continued availability of 2.0L MultiJet II for AWD variants
knownFor: Improved hybrid system reliability on post-2022 examples
Diesel variants remain popular for towing and high-mileage use
Parts availability strong for core MultiJet II engine codes
years.cta: Get quotes for 2022-2025 Jeep Compass replacement engines

faq.h2: Jeep Compass Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does it cost to replace a Jeep Compass engine in the UK?
items.answer: Typical rebuilt Jeep Compass engine prices range from £1,295-£3,200 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Supply & fit adds £450-£950 labour. Get multiple quotes to compare.
keyPoints: 1.6L MultiJet diesel: £1,295-£2,150 rebuilt supply-only
2.0L MultiJet diesel: £1,495-£2,850 rebuilt supply-only
1.4L MultiAir petrol: £1,350-£2,450 rebuilt supply-only
Hybrid variants cost more due to electrical complexity
items.cta: Compare rebuilt Jeep Compass engine prices for your variant

items.question: What are the most common Jeep Compass engine problems?
items.answer: Timing chain wear on 2.0L MultiJet diesel and MultiAir hydraulic unit failure on 1.4L petrol dominate UK replacement enquiries. DPF issues affect diesel models used for short journeys.
keyPoints: 2.0L MultiJet: Timing chain tensioner wear 60k-100k miles
1.4L MultiAir: Hydraulic solenoid failure causing misfires
Diesel models: DPF/EGR clogging on urban driving patterns
Hybrid models: 48V system electrical faults requiring specialist care
items.cta: Get quotes for Jeep Compass engine replacement specialists

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine on a Jeep Compass?
items.answer: Yes, if your Compass is in good condition otherwise. A rebuilt engine at £1,500-£3,000 often makes more financial sense than scrapping a £8,000-£15,000 vehicle.
keyPoints: Rebuilt engine cost typically 20-40% of vehicle value
12-month warranty provides peace of mind vs used engine
Consider variant desirability and mileage before deciding
Hybrid models may have higher replacement costs but retain value
items.cta: Find out if engine replacement makes sense for your Jeep Compass

items.question: Can I fit a used engine in my Jeep Compass?
items.answer: Yes, but verify compatibility carefully. Used Jeep Compass engines cost £895-£1,850 but require compression testing and may have hidden wear.
keyPoints: Always request compression test report before purchase
Verify engine code matches your variant exactly
Budget for ancillary parts replacement during fitment
Used engines typically carry shorter warranty than rebuilt
items.cta: Check used Jeep Compass engine availability in your area

items.question: What is the difference between rebuilt and reconditioned Jeep Compass engines?
items.answer: Rebuilt engines undergo comprehensive overhaul with upgraded components addressing known failures. Reconditioned units receive lighter refurbishment - suitable for budget replacements.
keyPoints: Rebuilt: All wear items replaced, upgraded components, 12-month warranty
Reconditioned: Key parts replaced, basic inspection, shorter warranty
Rebuilt recommended for high-mileage or failure-prone engines
Reconditioned suitable for low-mileage preventative replacement
items.cta: Compare rebuilt vs reconditioned Jeep Compass engine options

items.question: How long does a Jeep Compass engine last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: With regular servicing, Jeep Compass engines can reach 150,000+ miles. MultiJet diesel and Firefly petrol show better longevity than early MultiAir units.
keyPoints: 2.0L MultiJet diesel: 150k+ miles with timing chain attention
1.4L MultiAir petrol: 100k-120k miles typical before hydraulic issues
1.3L Firefly petrol: Early data suggests improved reliability
Hybrid variants: Battery life 8-10 years typical with proper care
items.cta: Get maintenance tips for extending your Jeep Compass engine life

items.question: Where can I find the engine code on my Jeep Compass?
items.answer: Your Jeep Compass engine code is stamped on the engine block near the gearbox, listed on the V5C logbook, and visible on the engine bay sticker.
keyPoints: Check V5C registration document section D.2 for engine code
Engine bay sticker near radiator often shows code
Block stamping visible with torch near gearbox housing
Enter registration on our site for instant code verification
items.cta: Locate your Jeep Compass engine code instantly

items.question: Do you supply and fit Jeep Compass engines nationwide?
items.answer: Yes, our UK network offers supply & fit service for Jeep Compass engines with mobile or garage fitting available across most regions.
keyPoints: Supply & fit adds £450-£950 to engine price depending on variant
Mobile fitting available in most UK postcodes
Hybrid and AWD models require specialist-equipped garages
All fitment includes post-installation diagnostics and road test
items.cta: Find Jeep Compass engine supply & fit deals near you

items.question: Can I upgrade my Jeep Compass to a more powerful engine?
items.answer: Engine swaps are possible but complex. Upgrading from 140ps to 170ps MultiJet requires ECU recalibration and may affect insurance - consult a specialist first.
keyPoints: 140ps to 170ps MultiJet swap possible with ECU adaptation
Petrol to diesel or vice versa requires extensive modification
Hybrid conversions not recommended due to electrical complexity
Always check insurance and MOT implications before upgrading
items.cta: Enquire about Jeep Compass engine upgrade compatibility

items.question: Does a replacement Jeep Compass engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, all engines supplied through EngineMarket include a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Terms vary by supplier - confirm details before ordering.
keyPoints: 12-month warranty standard on rebuilt/remanufactured units
Used engines typically carry 3-6 month warranty
Warranty covers parts and labour for manufacturing defects
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
items.cta: Compare warranty terms for Jeep Compass engine suppliers
